author: codex
scope: perf/tests lane follow-up only; no generated data edits and no materialize, validate, export-jsonl, DB repair/publish, or provider-backed commands

decision.summary: No additional patch is recommended. The recent materialize perf optimization and relation validator widening already have focused tests that cover the risky behavior directly.

materialize_perf_patch: materializableEntries now precomputes source-scoped local_observation_id sets instead of rebuilding a same-source Set for every accepted entry.
entriesToRecords now updates repeated local-id buckets in place instead of reconstructing Sets, and builds localIdsByRecord for canonical-id relation endpoint backfill.
MTA_MATERIALIZE_TIMING instrumentation was added around materialize phases but remains opt-in.
relation_validator_patch: has_metric now accepts claim -> metric_claim as well as event -> metric_claim.
The expected-shape error message for has_metric now includes claim in the subject-kind list.
Nearby already-landed relation rule widenings for source/event timeline and event claim carriers are covered in the same focused relation helper test.

targeted_checks_already_sufficient.test: does not materialize relations whose local endpoints exist only in another source
targeted_checks_already_sufficient.coverage: Guards the correctness side of the materializableEntries optimization: source-scoped endpoint presence must not be replaced by a global local_observation_id set.

targeted_checks_already_sufficient.test: keeps same-source relation endpoint filtering bounded for large accepted batches
targeted_checks_already_sufficient.coverage: Exercises 6,000 accepted relation submissions with missing endpoints and asserts entriesToRecords finishes under the focused threshold while filtering all invalid relations.

targeted_checks_already_sufficient.test: indexes repeated local observation ids without quadratic duplicate-set churn
targeted_checks_already_sufficient.coverage: Monkey-patches Set iteration to catch repeated local-id bucket reconstruction and verifies the repeated-local path stays bounded.

targeted_checks_already_sufficient.test: backfills relation local endpoint ids when canonical record ids are supplied directly
targeted_checks_already_sufficient.coverage: Covers the new localIdsByRecord backfill path for canonical-id relation endpoints.

targeted_checks_already_sufficient.test: prefers same-source local endpoint ids when backfilling merged canonical relation targets
targeted_checks_already_sufficient.coverage: Covers the source-preference branch in localIdsByRecord so merged canonical targets use same-source local ids when available.

targeted_checks_already_sufficient.test: accepts expected endpoint shapes and reports unexpected known shapes
targeted_checks_already_sufficient.coverage: Directly asserts relationEndpointShapeIssue("has_metric", "claim", "metric_claim") is accepted and that the has_metric expected-subject message includes claim.

commands_run.outcome: Confirmed the relevant source/test files were already modified in the shared worktree and review_notes contained untracked artifacts.
Located the materialize optimization, relation endpoint rule, and focused test coverage.
Reviewed the optimization implementation and materialize tests for same-source endpoint filtering, bounded batch behavior, repeated local-id indexing, and canonical-id endpoint backfill.
Verified has_metric claim -> metric_claim is in the endpoint-shape table and the focused relation helper test asserts acceptance plus the updated expected-shape message.
Reviewed the current focused diff; no obvious missing test gap remained.

commands_run.command: jq empty data/review_notes/codex-perf-test-followup-2026-06-22.json && jq '{decision_type: .decision.type, focused_check_count: (.targeted_checks_already_sufficient | length), final_commands: .commands_run[-2:]}' data/review_notes/codex-perf-test-followup-2026-06-22.json
commands_run.outcome: Post-write JSON parse passed; summary reported decision_type review_note_only and six focused checks.
Confirmed the review note is the only new file for this task.

residual_risk: The timing threshold in the 6,000-relation test is an environment-sensitive smoke guard, not a formal benchmark. It is useful for catching the previous quadratic behavior but should not be treated as a stable performance SLA.
The targeted tests exercise representative in-memory entriesToRecords paths; they do not replace a later serialized materialize/export/validate checkpoint in the generated-output lane.
The relation helper test proves the has_metric claim endpoint rule directly. It does not inspect live canonical backlog counts, which is intentionally outside this lane.
